meta.title: DROPP — The rhythm on your wrist. Your mind on care.
meta.description: Volume, duration: DROPP computes the drip rate, beats the tempo — before your eyes and on your wrist — and alerts you at the end. No account, no data collected. Coming soon to the App Store.
meta.ogDescription: Volume, duration: DROPP computes the drip rate, beats the tempo, and alerts you at the end. On iPhone and Apple Watch. Coming soon to the App Store.
meta.jsonLdDescription: Volume, duration: Dropp computes the infusion drip rate, beats the tempo on iPhone and Apple Watch, and alerts you at the end. No account, no data collected.

hero.soon: Coming soon to the App Store
hero.tagline:
The rhythm on your wrist.
Your mind on care.
hero.sub: Volume, duration: DROPP computes the drip rate, beats the tempo, and alerts you at the end.
hero.capWatchSub: A discreet tap for every drop.
hero.finalSub: DROPP keeps the rhythm and watches the clock.
hero.trust: No account · No ads · Data stays on device

demo.title: The whole gesture, at a glance.
demo.ariaLabel: Demonstration: enter, calculate, match, track, get alerted
demo.step3: 3 · Screen and Apple Watch beat the rhythm
demo.step4: 4 · Match the drip, then start tracking
demo.step4Hint: set the roller clamp to the tempo
demo.step6: 6 · The end reaches you — wherever you are

moment.item1: The formula you know by heart — redone between two call bells.
moment.item2: The clamp set by eye, recounted at the slightest doubt.
moment.item3: Several end times to hold in your head, all shift long.
moment.conclusion: It isn't a question of skill. It's a load — and DROPP carries it.

calc.title: Calculate
calc.body: Volume and duration instantly become drops per minute, seconds per drop and mL/h. Drop factors 10, 15, 20 and 60 gtt/mL — this demo is set to 20. Nothing to memorize, nothing to redo.
calc.warn: Outside the plausible range — the app refuses to beat an absurd tempo.

cal.title: Match
cal.body: The scene beats the computed tempo — visible, felt on the wrist, audible if you turn it on (a discreet tick, no louder than a click, that respects silent mode). Adjust the clamp until unison: when the drip chamber's drop falls together with DROPP's, you're there.
cal.moletteAria: Infusion roller clamp — adjust until unison

cont.title: Carry on
cont.body: Time remaining tracks itself. A pre-alert warns you five minutes before the end, and reminders adapt to the duration — a midpoint mark for a one-hour infusion, regular checkpoints for one that runs through the night. The end no longer rests on memory alone.

watch.title: Everything, without reaching for your iPhone.
watch.body: Start an infusion from the crown. Track it, pause it, find it by its label — Bay 3, Chair 5, Infusion A — without ever typing. Your wrist beats the tempo while you match the drip, and its alarms ring even if your iPhone stayed in your locker.

dt.title: One gesture, without touching the screen.
dt.body: Pinch your thumb and index finger twice: depending on the screen, DROPP starts the infusion, pauses it, resumes it, or acknowledges an alert. Hands full, hands gloved — the Apple Watch's native gesture answers at the wrist, no contact needed.
dt.caption: Start · Pause · Resume · Acknowledge

tap.name: Tap Mode
tap.title: Watch a rhythm. Tap it. Read it.
tap.body: Reproduce an observed rhythm by tapping the screen: DROPP shows its frequency per minute — no stopwatch, no mental math. For a drip rate, the reading also gives mL/h, enough to reset the clamp with confidence. On iPhone and on your wrist.
tap.hintStart: tap along with the rhythm you observe
tap.padAria: Tap area — tap along with the rhythm you observe

multi.name: Simultaneous infusions
multi.title: Every infusion: its label, its time, its alarm.
multi.note: Place labels, never names — Bay 3, Chair 5, Hallway 2. The label is optional.

pro.title: The Graphite theme — and everything that keeps watch with it.
pro.ariaLabel: Pro features shown in the dark Graphite theme: pre-alert and milestones, alarms on the wrist, local history, Premium 3D scene
pro.card1Body: A signal five minutes before the end, checkpoints adapted to the duration.
pro.card2Body: The Apple Watch alerts you, even if your iPhone stayed in your locker.
pro.card3Body: Infusions and readings stay on your device — nowhere else.
pro.card4Body: The same drop, with depth — the surface you see here is the app's, in Graphite.
pro.note: This panel uses the app's Graphite theme, included with Dropp Pro.

pricing.title: The essentials are free. Pro keeps up with your shift.
pricing.lifetimeNote: The lifetime purchase: everything, forever, no subscription.
pricing.freeNote: The end alarm is a safety signal: it will never be paywalled.

trust.t1Body: What you enter stays on your devices. DROPP has no technical means of accessing it.
trust.t2Body: Nothing to create, nothing to host, nothing to hack.
trust.t3Body: Basement, shielded room, home visits: DROPP works without a network.
trust.clinical: An aid for calculation, rhythm and tracking. It does not replace clinical judgment, local protocols, or control devices.

faq.a1: Unlimited calculations, the visual tempo, tracking for one infusion, and the end alarm — no ads, no account, no time limit.
faq.a2: Yes, entirely. Everything is computed and stored on the device; no connection is ever needed.
faq.q3: Is the iPhone required with the Apple Watch?
faq.a3: Yes — the Watch app comes with the iPhone app and installs through it. After that, wrist alarms ring even if the iPhone is off or out of range.
faq.a4: 10, 15, 20 and 60 drops per milliliter — the factor can be changed at any time in Settings.
faq.a5: Reproducing an observed rhythm by tapping the screen to read its frequency per minute — and, for a drip rate, its mL/h. No stopwatch, no mental math.
faq.q6: Does DROPP replace a pump or a control device?
faq.a6: No. DROPP is an aid for calculation, rhythm and tracking. It measures nothing, decides nothing, and substitutes for no device or protocol.
faq.a7: iOS 17 or later, watchOS 10 or later.

closing.title: Coming soon to the App Store.
closing.byline: Designed by a caregiver, proven on the floor — to keep attention where it matters.

js.tooSlow: the drip is too slow — open the clamp
js.tooFast: the drip is too fast — close the clamp
js.tapStart: tap along with the rhythm you observe
js.tapContinue: keep tapping at the same pace
js.tapTightening: per minute · the value is settling
